title: Qalculate
shortDescription: Mixed-unit math, built-in physical constants, and full Qalculate power — right in Command Palette.
description:
Bring the full power of Qalculate to your Command Palette workflow. Type expressions as you search and get instant results — then copy the answer with one keystroke.

**Mixed-unit math:** Do real arithmetic with incompatible units in one expression — `5 miles + 10 km`, `10 mph * 2 hours`, `(5 ft + 8 in) to cm` — then convert when you want. Most calculators make you convert everything first.

**Built-in constants:** Qalculate includes hundreds of physical, chemical, and mathematical constants you won't find on a typical calculator — use them by name in any expression, with automatic units:
- `FineStructure` — fine-structure constant α (≈ 1/137)
- `planck to eV s` — Planck constant in electron-volt seconds
- `dirac to eV s` — reduced Planck constant ħ
- `proton_mass to MeV/c^2` — proton mass in particle-physics units
- `PlanckLength to nm` — fundamental length scale
- `k_B to J/K` — Boltzmann constant
- `SpeedOfLight to mph` — speed of light, any units you want
- `molar_gas_constant * 298 K to J` — constants in real formulas

**More:** algebra, calculus, currency, dates, trigonometry, and simple conversions like `100 miles to km`.

**How it works**
Open Qalculate from Command Palette, type an expression, and results update live. Select a result to copy it to the clipboard.

**Requirements**
PowerToys with Command Palette enabled. Uses the Qalculate CLI (`qalc`). When installed via WinGet, Qalculate is installed automatically as a dependency.
